name: seren-loci
description: Gives GitHub Copilot and the user access to the SerenLoci left brain - set, get, search, and manage keyed coding/logic facts.

serenLoci.endpoint.description: Base URL of the SerenLoci service. Default port 7422 matches SerenLoci's own default.

serenLoci.startCommand.description: Shell command used to start the SerenLoci service when it is not reachable. Application-scoped so workspace settings cannot override it (security: prevents a malicious .vscode/settings.json from injecting a different command).

serenLoci.suppressStartPrompt.description: Suppress the 'service not reachable - start it?' prompt on extension activation. Use this if you intentionally run SerenLoci only for some projects.

serenLoci.bearerTokenHint.markdownDescription:
**Bearer token** is stored securely in the OS keychain, not here.

To set or update it, run the command:

`Seren Loci: Set Bearer Token` (via `Ctrl+Shift+P`)

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_set_fact
languageModelTools.userDescription: Set or replace a keyed fact in SerenLoci.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set (or replace) a fact in SerenLoci - the left brain of keyed, deterministic coding/logic knowledge. `project` defaults to '*' (fundamentals: cross-project truths like 'camelCase is life'); pass a concrete project name for a per-project convention. If a live value already exists for this (project, key) it is SUPERSEDED - kept as history, never blended into the new value. ALWAYS include `why` when there is one: it is the hard-won reason the fact is shaped this way, and it is searchable. Use this when the user states a durable preference, rule, or hard-won fact ('we use 4-space indent', 'this env var must be set at compile time').

key.description: The addressable locus - a dotted identifier like 'posh.brace_style' or 'cuda.no_vmm'.

why.description: The hard-won reason this fact is shaped this way. Searchable; include it whenever there is one.

project.description: Scope. '*' (default) = fundamentals (cross-project truths). A concrete project name = a per-project convention.

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_get_fact
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get THE live value for a key.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get THE live value for a (project, key), deterministically - exact address in, the thing out, no ranking, no guessing. This is the spine of the left brain. `project` defaults to '*' (fundamentals). Returns {found: false} if there is no live value (a never-set or retired key) - a clean answer, not an error. Use this when you know the exact key you want.

key.description: The exact key to look up (e.g. 'posh.brace_style').

project.description: Scope. '*' (default) = fundamentals. A concrete project name for a per-project value.

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_search
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search SerenLoci for relevant facts when you don't know the exact key.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search SerenLoci when you DON'T know the exact key - the associative jump ('that CUDA thing', 'the brace rule'). An exact key match leads at score 1.0; otherwise the finder runs (semantic vector search if an embedder is configured, else FTS5 lexical over key/value/why). Each hit carries a normalized 0..1 score and a match_kind. Call this BEFORE answering coding questions that might have a recorded project convention or fundamental rule. project=null (default) searches every scope; a concrete project narrows to it and folds in fundamentals unless you turn that off.

n_results.description: Max hits to return after ranking.

project.description: Narrow to a project scope. Omit to search every scope (fundamentals + all projects).

include_fundamentals.description: When a project is set, also fold in cross-project fundamentals.

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_forget_fact
languageModelTools.userDescription: Retire the live value for a key (kept as history).
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retire the live value for a (project, key) - a soft supersede, 'a flag, not a scalpel'. The row stays as history; it just stops being the live answer, and the key is free to be set fresh later. Returns ok:false if there was no live value to retire. Use when a fact is wrong or outdated, or the user asks to forget a recorded convention.

key.description: The key whose live value should be retired.

project.description: Scope. '*' (default) = fundamentals. A concrete project name for a per-project value.

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_history
languageModelTools.userDescription: Show every value a key has ever held.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Every value a (project, key) has ever held, newest first - the audit trail the strict-supersede rule preserves. Useful for 'what did we used to think the right answer was, and why did it change'. Empty list if the key is unknown.

key.description: The key to show the history of.

project.description: Scope. '*' (default) = fundamentals. A concrete project name for a per-project value.

languageModelTools.name: seren_loci_list_facts
languageModelTools.userDescription: List the facts in a scope.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List facts in scope - a whole project, or everything (omit project). For surveying what the left brain already knows before setting new facts or answering broadly. Pass include_superseded=true to fold in history rows too.

project.description: Narrow to a project scope. Omit to list every scope.

include_superseded.description: Include superseded (historical) rows, not just live facts.
